sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés, currently sitting at 16th in the league with 35 points, hosts Atlético Madrid, who are in an impressive 3rd position with 67 points, on May 3, 2025, at 12:00 PM. This Round 34 clash at Estadio de Mendizorroza in Vitoria-Gasteiz promises to be a challenging encounter for the home side. With referee Juan Martinez Munuera overseeing the game, known for averaging 4.8 yellow cards and 0.21 red cards per match, discipline will be key for both teams.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: In their last meeting on November 23, 2024, Atlético Madrid secured a 2-1 victory at home against Deportivo Alavés. Antoine Griezmann was the standout player, earning an 8.1 Sofascore rating as the Player of the Match. Looking at the last 5 head-to-head matchups, Atlético Madrid holds the edge with 3 wins compared to Alavés' 2, with no draws recorded. This historical dominance could play on the minds of the home team as they prepare for this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Deportivo Alavés: Team Form and Key Players
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have struggled defensively, conceding 46 goals this season, ranking 13th in the league for least goals conceded. Offensively, they've scored 35 goals, also ranking 13th, with key contributions from forward Kike García, who has netted 12 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 8.49 by 3.51. Midfielder Carlos Vicente, ranked 5th in the league for key passes with 57, has provided 5 assists, while Joan Jordán, with a league rank of 5 for percentage of long balls (8.8%), adds creativity with 4 goals and 2 assists. However, their team streaks show a concerning trend of games with less than 2.5 goals in their last 5 matches, indicating a lack of firepower.

sections.subtitle: Atlético Madrid: Team Strength and Star Power
sections.text: Atlético Madrid come into this game with a potent attack, led by Julián Álvarez, who has scored 15 goals, ranking 4th in the league, and overperforming his expected goals of 12.41 by 2.59. Alexander Sørloth has also been clinical with 13 goals, while Antoine Griezmann continues to shine with 8 goals and 6 assists. Defender Axel Witsel stands out with a league-leading 73.9% ground duels won, showcasing their defensive solidity. In form over the last 5 matches, Álvarez has an average Sofascore rating of 7.5 with 4 goals, making him a major threat. However, their streak of being the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 away games could give Alavés hope.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés excel in tackles per game, leading the league with 19.12, and rank 5th in interceptions per game at 8.12, suggesting a robust defensive setup. They also rank 2nd in penalty area entries per game with 30.74, hinting at their ability to create chances despite a low conversion rate. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, score heavily late in games, with 25.93% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Alavés must be wary of late surges, as they also score 30.43% of their goals in the same period.

sections.subtitle: Standout Historical Performer
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Atlético Madrid, Alavés goalkeeper Antonio Sivera has been a consistent performer with an average Sofascore rating of 7.5. Despite not scoring or assisting, his 3.6 saves per game across 5 starts highlight his importance in keeping games close against a formidable opponent. His presence could be crucial in frustrating Atlético’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Discipline and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Discipline will be a concern for both sides, as head-to-head stats show more than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 encounters, aligning with Alavés’ streak of over 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7 home games. Alavés also lead the league in fouls committed with 556 and have received 95 yellow cards, ranking 18th for least yellows. Corners are likely to be limited, with both teams showing a trend of less than 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 9 head-to-heads, and Alavés averaging 4.62 corners per game, ranking 12th in the league.
